61.reason: [The index i is incremented before null-terminating, which skips one position and may cause buffer overflow or incorrect string termination. It should be c[i+1]='\0;' or use c[i]='\0;' after the loop without incrementing i first.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The index i is incremented before null-terminating, which skips one position and may cause buffer overflow or incorrect string termination. It should be c[i+1]='\0;' or use c[i]='\0;' after the loop without incrementing i first.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The index i is incremented before null-termination, causing the string to be null-terminated one position too far. This leads to an uninitialized character being included in the string and potential buffer overflow if i reaches maxword-1. It should be c[i+1]='\0;' or better: after loop, set c[i]='\0;' without incrementing i first.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [off-by-one error: after loop, i is index of last letter; incrementing before '\0' skips one position, possibly causing buffer overflow or incorrect string]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [off-by-one error: after reading last letter, i points to last valid index; incrementing before null-terminator writes beyond intended length and may exceed maxword buffer]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Buffer overflow: 'i' may reach 'maxword-1', then '++i' makes it 'maxword', writing '\0' beyond array bounds]

45.reason: [char ch should be int to properly handle EOF, as fgetc returns int]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [char ch should be int to properly handle EOF, as fgetc returns int]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [char type is too small to hold EOF; should be int]

47.reason: [fgetc returns int; storing in char may cause EOF comparison to fail on systems where char is unsigned]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [fgetc returns int; storing in char may cause EOF comparison to fail on systems where char is unsigned]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [fgetc returns int, but ch is char; this causes EOF comparison to fail on systems where char is unsigned]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [same issue: ch is char, but fgetc returns int; may misinterpret EOF or high-bit characters]
